symptoms.character: swollen, tight, puffy calf, worse than the other leg

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute unilateral limb swelling is a core feature of DVT.
Swelling can occur with a calf tear but usually follows exertion/trauma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constant deep throbbing calf pain, worse on weight-bearing
symptoms.character: constant deep throbbing ache, severe, cannot bear weight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep, aching calf pain worse on standing is typical of DVT.
Warmth and erythema over a swollen calf accompany DVT.
Warmth/erythema also occur in cellulitis, but here without fever or a skin breach.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent commencement of a combined (oestrogen-containing) oral contraceptive pill

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exogenous oestrogen is a major acquired VTE provoking factor, greatest in the first months of use.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strong family history of recurrent lower-limb thrombosis (mother)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A strong family history of thrombosis raises suspicion for an inherited thrombophilia (e.g. Factor V Leiden).

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, dyspnoea or haemoptysis at present

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of PE symptoms lowers the probability of a current embolus but does NOT exclude it or reduce the propagation risk — mandatory safety-net item.

symptoms.symptom_name: No preceding trauma, exertion, long-haul travel or surgery

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No athletic trauma or exertion makes a muscle tear unlikely.
An otherwise unprovoked event in a thrombophilic, oestrogen-exposed patient fits an inherited-thrombophilia DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Calf circumference 4 cm greater than the contralateral leg (measured 10 cm below tibial tuberosity)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A >3 cm calf-circumference difference is a scored item on the Wells DVT criteria — sealed clinician measurement, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep popliteal/calf tenderness and positive Homan's sign; distal pulses intact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep tenderness supports DVT; Homan's sign is non-specific. Clinician examination finding — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vital signs essentially normal (afebrile, no tachycardia, normal oxygen saturation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal vitals do NOT exclude DVT or a subsequent PE — reassurance from them is a documented pitfall. Clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Venous duplex (compression) ultrasound of the left leg

investigation_details.expected_result: Non-compressible proximal vein confirming deep vein thrombosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: DVT is neither confirmed nor excluded; propagation to PE risk is unaddressed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compression ultrasound is the confirmatory first-line test — only obtainable in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Factor V Leiden genetic testing / thrombophilia screen

investigation_details.investigation_name: Thrombophilia screen including Factor V Leiden mutation analysis
investigation_details.expected_result: Heterozygous/homozygous Factor V Leiden mutation (activated protein C resistance)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Underlying inherited thrombophilia not characterised; deferred until after the acute event and off the interference of acute thrombosis/anticoagulation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Suspected Factor V Leiden thrombophilia (inherited activated protein C resistance)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Genetic testing confirms the inherited cause — a laboratory test, never available in real time via telehealth, and deferred to after the acute phase.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute unilateral swollen/warm/tender calf plus fresh oestrogen exposure plus a strong family history of thrombosis is a high pretest-probability DVT that mandates urgent objective testing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous compression duplex ultrasound of the left leg
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line confirmatory test for suspected DVT; must be arranged urgently in person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: D-dimer (as part of ED VTE work-up)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports risk stratification within the ED pathway; not a telehealth-obtainable test.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thrombophilia screen / Factor V Leiden genetic testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deferred until after the acute event and anticoagulation decisions — NOT ordered acutely.
